message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_pdist autograd via index_select composition for finite p > 0 [bd-0i8u]

tensor_pdist used to extract values, compute pairwise distances in
plain f64, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — silently
severing autograd. PyTorch's torch.nn.functional.pdist IS
differentiable for finite p > 0 (sub-gradient at the abs corner).

Strategy for finite p > 0:
  build row_i / row_j index tensors of length N*(N-1)/2 enumerating
   upper-triangular pairs (i, j) with i < j
  left  = index_select(input, 0, row_i)   shape [out_len, m]
  right = index_select(input, 0, row_j)   shape [out_len, m]
  diff  = left - right
  per_pair = (sum_k |diff[k]|^p)^(1/p)  via abs + pow + sum_dim + pow

All ops autograd-aware so gradients flow to input. p == 0
(counting metric, non-diff) and p == +inf (max-routing, deferred)
now fail-loud on requires_grad=true rather than silently severing.

Adds pdist_l2_propagates_gradient_through_input regression test:
  x = [[0, 0], [3, 4]], p=2 → d_01 = 5
  Under d_01-as-loss: grad = [[-3/5, -4/5], [3/5, 4/5]]   (asserts to 1e-12)

Closes frankentorch-0i8u. ft-api lib tests: 1169 passing (+1).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
